text:
"Your reliance on Dhahabi and Ibn Hajar collapses.

Both Dhahabi and Ibn Hajar mention that al-Amashs usage of  from Abu Salih has weakness.

When a scholar mentions Jarh in one place and Tadlis in another, the Jarh supersedes the Tadil. 

Dhahabi in his Al-Muhadhdhab fi-Ikhtisar al-Sunan al-Kabir (abridgement of al-Bayhaqis Sunan al-Kabir) says when mentioning a narration of al-Amash using  Abu Salih:

"And this hadith was not heard by al A'mash from Abu Salih."

[Dhahabis Al-Muhadhdhab fi-Ikhtisar al-Sunan al-Kabir 1/425 Dar al-Watan Edition]

Ibn Hajar in Al Nukat ala Sahih al-Bukhari says:

"And al-Tirmidhi transmitted it and said: Hasan. He said:
'We did not call it sahih because it is said that al A'mash practiced tadlis in it, saying: "I was told on the authority of () Abu Salih."'

I say: However, in the narration of Muslim, from Abu Usamah, from al A'mash: Abu Salih narrated to us (), so the accusation of his tadlis is removed."

[Ibn Hajar in Al Nukat ala Sahih al-Bukhari 2/107 Al-Maktabah al-Islamiyyah Edition]

So according to Ibn Hajar the accusation of tadlis was only lifted in that particular narration (a different one to the narration of this threads topic) only due to direct hearing mentioned for it elsewhere.
Otherwise the accusation of tadlis is there."

text:
"A Reminder of Fairness and Justice in Defending the People of Knowledge
 
Among the first to be targeted with attacks and assaults on his status by Amjad Rafq was the noble Shaykh, the Faqh Araft al-Muammad (may Allh preserve him).
 
By the praise of Allh, his advice and refutations bore fruit, and today you can see the reality of what he had warned against, in what Amjad Rafq is now displaying of transgressions towards the status of knowledge and its people.
 
I wish to draw attention to a matter that may go unnoticed in the writings of some of the brothers and callers in the West:
 
Despite what we see, and it is commendable, of their responses to Amjad Rafqs attacks on Shaykh al-Allmah Abdullh al-Bukhr (may Allh preserve him), we hardly see a similar defence of their brother, Shaykh Araft al-Muammad (may Allh preserve him).
 
Even though Amjad Rafq had previously attacked him, accused him of lying, attributed to him weakness in knowledge, and described him with offensive terms, many of them did not move to refute this falsehood or stop this transgression.
 
It would have been more fitting, in upholding justice and fairness, that they also defend the honour of Shaykh Araft (may Allh preserve him), as he was among the first to confront the errors of Amjad Rafq and warn against his methodology.

Written by: Jafar al-Hshim al-Awz
@Arafatbinhassan @ZubayrAbbasi @yasin_ibn_jamal @AbooSuhailah"

text:
""How the Salaf would read Quran

Shaykh Abdussalam as-Shuwayer   
"
--------------

How Often Should I Read the Qurn?
 
Shaykh Muammad bin Al bin dam al-Ityb   said:

 this shows that, at the very least, reading the [entire] Qurn should be done monthly; every night, you should read one juz until you complete it at the end of the month. This is the longest period of time it should be. Then, if you have desire, in twenty, fifteen, and so on, and do not do it more than every seven days; seven days was the standard of the Salaf: the majority of the Salaf used to read the Qurn in seven parts; they split it into seven portions, and so they would finish it every week.

Tr: Ab Hurayrah al-Birn

text:
"Al-Fudayl ibn Iyad, may Allah have mercy on him, said:
If you see oppressors taking revenge on other oppressors, stand and observe in astonishment.

When you see the greatest enemies of Islam in this era, those with the most blood of Muslims on their hands, turning against one another and weakening themselves through their conflicts, let your heart be soothed and turn to Allah in gratitude.

Sometimes, Allah soothes hearts by letting oppressors and tyrants orchestrate their own downfall through their conflicts and wars.

We make the wrongdoers destructive to one another because of their misdeeds. (6:129)

The parties involved in this war, the US along with their awliy, the leaders of the Gulf, as well as the Rafidah and the Yahud, are enemies, equally guilty and steeped in crimes against Islam and Muslims. Their hands are stained with the blood of countless innocents, and to true believers who grasp the depth of their kufr and the enormity of their crimes, each is as condemned as the next, none surpassing the other in guilt. They vied ruthlessly in their oppression and in betraying the Muslims.

Malik ibn Dinar, may Allah have mercy on him, said:
"I read in the revealed Books of Allah that Allah, the Exalted, said: I destroy My enemies through My enemies, and then I destroy them through My awliy."

May Allah prolong their strife so that they are preoccupied with one another and weakened, and may He raise to leadership on this earth the Muslims they betrayed, oppressed, and harmed, so that Tawheed may be established across the earth!

May Allah safeguard every innocent Muslim wherever this conflict occurs!

Ahmad Musa Jibril
2 Shawwal 1447 AH"

text:
Doubt: Shaykh Muqbil ibn Haadi, was a takfiri or made Takfir on Saudi

Responded by his student, Shaykh AbdulHameed az-Zukuri  :

Yahya Silmi As-Saylani (the Sri Lankan) says:
Sheikh Muqbils initial opinion, which caused a great uproar, was: The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia is a kafir country. He was firmly set on this statement, announcing it in Friday sermons. Then, when he went to Saudi Arabia before his death, he sat with its scholars, and they convinced him that it is a Salafi country, so he retracted and acknowledged that it is a pure Muslim country. [From the tape: Explanation of My Advice to Ahlus-Sunnah].

Sheikh Abdulhamid Az-Zukari responds:
So on Friday, the twenty-fifth of Rajab, in the year 1433 AH, we respond to this false statement, falsely and aggressively attributed to our sheikh, our father, and our teacher, Imam Muqbil ibn Hadi Al-Wadii, may Allah Almighty have mercy on himthe one whom Allah, may He be glorified and exalted, blessed and favoured by making him a reviver of this religion alongside other revivers, such as Sheikh Al-Albani, may Allah have mercy on him, Sheikh Ibn Baz, may Allah have mercy on him, and Sheikh Al-Uthaymin, may Allah have mercy on him. We have spoken about his way, his methodology, his biography, and some of what he adhered to in our book Al-Bayan Al-Hasan fi Tarjamat Al-Imam Al-Wadii wa Ma Ahyahu min As-Sunan. And we have responded to those who claim that Sheikh Muqbil, may Allah Almighty have mercy on him, was a takfiri at any time or period in the book Awn Al-Bari li Bayan Hizbiyyati Ibnay Mari wa Man Jara Majrahum wa Radd Takharusat Abdullah Al-Bukhari. In that book, we explained the gist: that Sheikh Muqbil, may Allah Almighty have mercy on him, was never a Haruri or a Khariji; rather, he was a Sunni Salafi ever since Allah, may He be glorified and exalted, blessed him with the knowledge of the creed of the Salaf, Ahlus-Sunnah wal-Jamaah. Thus, he became a caller to it and a reviver of it. 

Among the evidence that shows the falsehood of Yahya Silmis statementthat Sheikh Muqbil, may Allah Almighty have mercy on him, said about the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia that it is a kafir countryare the following matters:

First: Sheikh Muqbil, may Allah Almighty have mercy on him, refuted Osama ibn Laden, refuted Abu Muhammad Al-Maqdisi, refuted Al-Masari, and spoke against Salman, Safar, and those similar to them who hold takfiri statements and deviant methodologies.

Second: We are certain and convinced that our sheikh, may Allah have mercy on him, never said this statement at any time because it contradicts reality, and Sheikh Muqbil was among the people most aware of the condition of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabiamay Allah grant it success and protect it with Tawhid.

Third: Sheikh Muqbil, may Allah have mercy on him, did not apply this description to countries that are less upright and less devoted to spreading good, let alone countries containing graves and shrines worshipped besides Allah, despite his belief that those who engage in grave-worship, those who call upon another god alongside Allah, those who slaughter for other than Allah, and those who make vows for other than Allah are disbelievers.

Fourth: Such an absolute statement is questionable. Even if we were to suppose that in that country there are acts of disbelief, or in Yemen there are acts of disbelief, or in Sudan there are acts of disbelief, or in Iraq there are acts of disbelief, this kind of absolute statementcalling any of the Muslim countries a kafir country outrightis questionable. This claim is contradicted by Sheikh Muqbils tapes, contradicted by the actual stance of Sheikh Muqbil, and contradicted by the students of Sheikh Muqbil.

Fifth: If Sheikh Muqbil had ever uttered this statement or something similar, the scholars would have condemned him for it.

text:
To add one more thing: Iranian nationalists always ignore is a map they can never answer. The average Iranian is not even aware of this basic fact, and once you see it, the fairy tale of a uniquely ordained Iran with natural borders uniting Aryans since antiquity collapses. But as a starter, Iranian speaking peoples did not even originate on the Iranian plateau at all. They migrated into it. Before them, the Elamites were already there, non Iranian in language and identity, with a civilisation LONG predating the arrival of Iranians. The same applies to Kurds, who long predate the modern Iranian state and were later absorbed into a Persian nationalist project they never consented to.
The geology alone destroys the myth. The Arabian plate penetrates deep into what is now called Iran. Ahwaz, historically known as Arabistan even under the Safavids, sits on the Arabian plate, not the Iranian microcontinent. That is why its climate, landscape, and historical orientation align with Mesopotamia and Arabia, not the Persian heartlands. This is not ideology. It is geology. These are factsz simple as.
The same fantasy is imposed on Kurdish lands. The Zagros was never a sacred Persian boundary. It was a zone of movement and autonomy for Kurds and other highland peoples, later subdued through conquest and centralisation.

So when Iranian nationalists talk about natural borders, what they really mean is simple. These are territories they conquered early enough that they now pretend geography itself demanded it. This is not destiny. It is conquest rewritten as nature.,50.0

text:
"The Danger of Free-Mixing and a Warning to Those Who Treat the Matter Lightly

Ab Hurayrah narrated that the Messenger of Allh (   ) said:

The best rows for men are the first rows, and the worst are the last rows, and the best rows for women are the last rows, and the worst are the first rows. Muslim 440.

Al-Nawaw comments:

The reason the last rows are preferred for women who are present with men is because they are farther from mixing with the men, seeing them, becoming attached in the heart due to seeing their movements or hearing their speech, and similar matters. For this reason, the first rows are considered the opposite (less preferable) for women, and Allh knows best.

If this concern applies even in the most sacred of places, where hearts are softened, desires are restrained by the fear of Allh, and one is surrounded by righteous believers, yet unnecessary mixing between men and women is still cautioned against, then what about places of lesser sanctity, where temptation is stronger, heedlessness is more common, and restrain is weaker?

Munb al-ml"

text:
"Somali and foreign IS-Somalia leadership still remain entrenched in the Puntland mountains.

As shown in recently released ISIS media, the footage features Abu Ammar al-Maghribi, a senior figure within ISIS-Somalia, who was present in the Cal Miskaad mountain range in the Bari region. For some time, conflicting reports suggested that much of the groups leadership had withdrawn from these mountains following clashes with Puntland security forces and U.S. airstrikes. However, credible intelligence indicates that the core leadership of ISIS-Somalia remains in place.

In particular, Abdulkadir Mumin, the groups leader, is believed to still be operating in the area alongside several of his closest associates. It is also assessed that hundreds of foreign fighters remain in the region, determined to continue fighting despite heavy losses. Many have been killed, while others have surrendered or been captured, including some now in the custody of Puntland authorities.

Stay tuned and follow @Wargelinn for further updates.

Its very unlikely the shaykh was given the proper details if he came to this conclusion because SFE isnt selling education, the university is. SFE is onlyp providing financing: it pays the university and immediately creates a liability on the student for the amounts paid (regardless of whether they drop out or not), with interest included from the day of payment to the university. Its got all the hallmarks of a repayable debt/loan (from a fiqh pov) so calling it a purchase of education from SFE wouldnt actually make sense.

text:
"In defense of Shaykh Ibn Jibrn by the Grand Muft, Shaykh li al-Fawzn.

Q: May Allh bless you, this questioner says that some students of knowledge were happy at the death of Shaykh Ibn Jibrn, may Allh have mercy on him. Is this action correct?

Ans: This is not permissible. Rejoicing at the death of a Muslim in general is not permissible, because he is someone who has entered the protection of Islm, so how much more at the death of a scholar? How much more at the death of a scholar! It is not permissible for them to rejoice at the death of a scholar from the scholars of the Muslims. This only happens either out of ignorance, or because of hypocrisy, and we seek refuge with Allh. Yes.

Q: May Allh preserve you, O Shaykh, we see now on some social media platforms that some brothers, may Allh guide them, we see them speaking against major scholars such as Shaykh Abdullh ibn Jibrn, may Allh have mercy on him, and Shaykh Abd al-Ramn al-Barrk, may Allh preserve him and prolong his life, attributing to them things Allh has sent down no authority for. They follow up their slips and say, These people said such-and-such, so their intention must be such-and-such. What is your guidance, may Allh preserve you, especially since they have many followers on these platforms?

Ans: This is not permissible at all. This is backbiting, from the forbidden backbiting, and backbiting the scholars is even more severe, and we seek refuge with Allh. It is not permissible to backbite the scholars or speak against them or follow up their mistakes. You must advise such people if you see them, or if any one of them is among you, then advise him regarding this. As the scholars have said: The flesh of the scholars is poisoned, their flesh is poisoned. So it is not permissible to backbite them or speak against them.

Q: May Allh be good to you, this question has been repeated often: there are those who attack some of the shaykhs, such as Shaykh Abdullh ibn Jibrn, may Allh have mercy on him, and describe him with inappropriate descriptions. What is your direction for such people, and what is your advice for those who have made wounding the scholars and supplicators their occupation?

Ans: May Allh be good to you, these people harm no one but themselves; they do not harm the mashyakh. They harm none but themselves. Even if we suppose that some of the shaykhs have some error, if he is alive, he is advised; and if he is dead, he is asked forgiveness for, because he is your brother. You seek forgiveness for him and ask Allh to grant him forgiveness, reward, and recompense. Yes. May Allh be good to you.
 
Source:
 

We advise our brothers to fear Allh and to stop circulating claims that are baseless. They should also avoid exaggerating the status of certain individuals by giving them titles they neither merit nor have attained. This kind of ghuluw is harmful, and it only creates the false impression that the one making criticism is a scholar of tremendous authority, when that is simply not the case. Shaykh Muqbil, may Allh reward him for what he contributed, had his efforts in the field of adth and his role in reviving study among many students. However, he was not free of shortcomings, and it is well-known that his knowledge in usl al-fiqh, fiqh, and the broader Islamic sciences was limited compared to the major scholars of his time. Therefore, calling him Shaykh al-Islm is an exaggeration without evidence. In truth, Shaykh Ibn Jibrn, may Allh have mercy on him, was far ahead of him in jurisprudence, its principles, and in many other fields of knowledge. May Allh honor our scholars, reward them for their efforts, forgive their mistakes, and protect us from statements that cause division or unnecessary hostility within the Salaf community. mn."

""The Prophet  said: If you have no shame then do as you wish 

Would Menk be happy with his daughter dancing with this man? Would he be thinking of the lyrics they dance to? 

Then why is it ok for the rest of the daughters of the Ummah? 

@muftimenk acts with impunity and no shame by endorsing the lyrics of an open sinner, a singer who dances with women in public. 

This is a sick and deranged version of Ill be kind to everyone 

Just like Shareea along with hikma dictates when and where to be harsh, likewise; kindness and tolerance is measured.

May Allah guide this man and stop him from his despicable actions."
"
--------------

Alhamdulillah a much needed response, may Allah reward you.

Many people will reply with "Why waste your time... why are you judging others..." 

Read below, and you will realise why this response was necessary:

Ab Idrs al-Khawln ( ):

              .

For me to see a blazing fire in the masjid is more beloved than to see an innovation (bidah) in it that has not been changed.

[Al-Shar wa al-Ibnah: 254]

Shaykh al-Islam Ibn Taymiyyah ( ) said:

"The one who refutes the innovators is a mujahid."

Al-Awz   said:

If an innovation becomes apparent and the people of knowledge do not repudiate it, then it is [perceived as] a Sunnah!

[Sharaf Ashb Al-Hadth: 1/22]

Ibn Abbs    said:

A man from Ahlus-Sunnah calls to the Sunnah and forbids from innovation!

[Sharh Usl I'tiqd: No.11]

text:
"Who was ash-Shaykh, al-Allmah, Ubayd al-Jbir?

He is the Shaykh and Allmah, Ubayd bin Abd Allh bin Sulaymn al-Jbir al-Hamadn, and Ban Jbir is among the tribes of arb of the ijz.
He was born in the town of al-Faqr in al-Far valley in the region of Madnah in 1357 AH (1938). At the age of eight, he moved with his father to Mahd al-Dhahab, where he acquired the first stages of his education. His father moved there in order to work. He remained in education at this location for eight years. After that, he returned to al-Far valley. Then in 1376 AH (1956) he settled in Madnah and, due to family circumstances, was unable to study for a period of time.

Then in the year 1381 AH (1961), he resumed his studies, starting with Dr al-adth al-Madaniyyah. Then he joined al-Mahad al-Ilm and stayed there for five years. He got married before joining the Faculty of Sharah in the Islamic University of Madnah in 1388 AH (1968). He graduated four years later in 1392 AH (1972), excelling in his grade.
From his teachers through the course of his studies include: Shaykh Sayf al-Ramn bin Amad, Shaykh Ammr bin Abd Allh, Shaykh Abd al-Ramn bin Abd Allh al-Ajln, Shaykh umayd al-zim, Shaykh ammd al-Anr and Shaykh Abd al-Musin al-Abbad.

After that, he was an imm in Masjid al-Sabt in Madnah between 1387-1392 AH. Then he was a teacher at Umar bin Abd al-Aziz Intermediate School in Jeddah from 1392 AH to 1396 AH. After that, the Shaykh worked as a caller for Markaz al-Dawah wal-Irshd, assisting the director of the centre, Shaykh Abd al-Azz bin Bz, in his presence and acting on his behalf in his absence from the end of 1396 AH to 1404 AH (1976-1983).
Then he was a teacher at the Islmic University between the end of 1407 AH to 1414 AH. During his presence there, he also attained a Masters in the subject of tafsr. After a few years serving at the Community Service Centre affiliated with the Islmic University, the Shaykh then retired in 1417 AH (1996).

After this the Shaykh continued his lectures and lessons, with students from the Islmic University frequenting his home and benefiting from his knowledge, advice and wisdom. During this period, the Shaykh also aided the Salafi dawah tremendously, particularly in the West by way of fatherly advice, clarifications, admonitions and refutations of the people of falsehood and their false innovated principles. The Shaykh had a very strong relationship with the students of knowledge from Maktabah Salafiyyah, Birmingham. The Shaykh has many beneficial works in the field of aqdah, manhaj, and fiqh. Shaykh Rab bin Hd (may Allh have mercy on him) said of the Shaykh: Shaykh Ubayd is from the esteemed scholars of the Salafs, and he is known for piety, abstinence and speaking with truth.

Shaykh Ubayd was also praised and commended by other Major Scholars such as Shaykh Amad al-Najm and Shaykh Zayd al-Madkhal (may Allh have mercy on them).
The Shaykh (may Allh have mercy on him) passed away at the age of 84 on 28 Rab al-Thn 1444 AH, corresponding to 22 November 2022. May Allh shower him with mercy and reward him greatly for his service to Islm, the Muslims and the Salaf dawah.

Some of this great scholars works:

text:
""Why do people encourage people to speak about things, even if they may have no knowledge about it? If you want to make a difference, you dont make the whole world speak. You ask the people with knowledge to speak"
"
--------------

This brother made a very good point .
Most of us dont even know the entirety of juz Amma , what makes you think you can discuss the differences between Ahkaam and aqidah ?

It is known from our pious predecessors that the foundation of gaining ilm was to memorise the entire Quran before studying rullings , ilmul Hadith , jarh wa tadeel etc  . So if you cannot do that then stay silent until you have done so .

Theres a famous story between imam Abu hanifah and Imam Muhammad Bin Hassan Ash-Shaybaani. 
Ash-Shaybaani wanted to enter and sit with Abu Hanifah during his duroos . But Abu Hanfiah had stipulated a condition and that was to first go memorise the Quran and then come back once he had completed . 
Imam Muhammad was so eager that he memorised the words of Allah  entirely in just 8 days .

So, if you want to succeed as a student of knowledge, do Hifz Ul-Quran , then continue with your further studies . You cannot master one and leave the other out , they both go hand in hand . 

May Allah grant us to be great Huffz and allow the words of Allah to enter our hearts

text:
"This video serves as an example of the importance of staying in your lane. 

      
Whoever speaks in matters external to his field ends up producing strange positions 

In this video, this speaker has ostracised millions of Muslims who trace their creed back to the time of the pious predecessors. 
He speaks as if the Salaf creed was born with Sh. Muhammad bin Abd al-Wahhb, whilst any basic student of Aqdah is aware that scholars of this creed predate Ibn Abd al-Wahhb by circa a thousand years. Even those who critique this creed and claim that it doesnt represent the Aqdah of the Salaf acknowledge the point that it predates Ibn Abdil Wahhb, citing Ibn Taymiyyahs creed as a prime example (d. 728AH). 

Furthermore, his categorisation of acceptable creeds into Ashar, Mturd and the Tahw creed is bizarre for multiple reasons. First, he argues that these creeds are the Aqidat al-Salaf, whilst ignoring & sidelining the creed of the Imm of Ahlus Sunnah, Ahmad bin Hanbal. Was his creed not the creed of the Salaf? Second, his mentioning of the Tahw creed as the third creed adds to his blunders. Both the Ashirah and the Mturdyyah would claim that the creed of al-Tahw reflects their Aqdah, as would the Athars. Specialists in this field from each of these schools do not treat the Tahw creed as a separate school of Aqdah. 

As for his immature, ridiculous comments like you have a mind of a nine-year-old and you have some IQ in your head?  they do not deserve a response. Such statements only reflect badly upon the one who says them. 

This is just a brief response; much more could be said on this mess of a video.",

Rather, it is upon usthose who recognize his sincerity and his service to the truthto rise and defend his honor. When lies are spread and distortions are weaponized, remaining silent becomes a betrayal to the Shaykh, more-so to a brother in Deen.

- Those cheerleading this attack on Iran, although most casualties are innocent civilians, including Sunnis.

- Those who try to water down all the crimes and shirk of the Rawafid.

text:
Truth Before Ties

Adh-Dhahabi, in his biography of Al-Husayn ibn Abi As-Surri Al-'Asqalani, said: Abu 'Arubah Al-Harrani said:
    . 
 He (Al-Husayn) is the maternal uncle of my mother, and he is a liar. [Mzn Al-Itidl (1/536)] 

Adh-Dhahabi, in his biography of Yay ibn Abi Unaysah Al-Jazari, said: Ubaydullh ibn Amr said: Zayd ibn Abi Unaysah told me: 
     .  
Do not write from my brother, for he is a liar. [Mzn Al-Itidl (4/364)] 

And he said in the biography of Al-Husayn ibn Abi As-Surri Al-Asqalani: Abu Dwd declared him weak. And his brother Muhammad said: 
     . 
Do not write from my brother, for he is a liar. [Mzn al-Itidl (1/536)] 

Adh-Dhahabi, may Allah have mercy on him, said in the biography of Abdullh ibn Jafar ibn Naj, the father of Al ibn Al-Madn: Ibn Al-Madn said: 
 . 
My father is weak. [Mzn al-Itidl (2/401)] 

Imam Abu Dwd Sulaymn ibn Al-Ashath said about his son Abdullh:
 . 
My son is a liar. [Mzn al-Itidl (2/433)] 

Adh-Dhahabi clarified: As for his fathers statement about him, it appears that if it is authentically attributed to him, he meant that he was a liar in his everyday speech, not regarding prophetic adth. It seems he said this when Abdullh was still a young man, but later he grew older and became a leading scholar. [Tadhkirat Al-uffdh (2/238)] 

Adh-Dhahabi said in the biography of Sad ibn Shubah ibn Al-ajjj:
 .
He is truthful.

Shubah said: 
      .
I named my son Sad, but he neither succeeded nor prospered. [Mzn Al-Itidl (2/122)] 

Adh-Dhahabi said about his son Ab Hurayrah:
       .
He memorised the Quran, then preoccupied himself with other matters until he forgot it. [Fat Al-Mughth (4/356)] 

Adh-Dhahabi said in the biography of Hishm ibn assn, Ab Abdillh Al-Qards:
   
He is trustworthy, a great and eminent Imam. Ibn Ad said: Amad ibn Muammad ibn Shabb narrated to us: Amad ibn Asad narrated to us: Shuayb ibn arb narrated to us: I heard Shubah say: 
           . 
If I were to show favoritism to anyone, it would be to Hishm ibn assnhe was my brother-in-lawbut he did not have strong memory. [Mzn Al-Itidl (4/495496)] 

Imam Muslim mentions in the introduction to his a:
ajjj ibn Ash-Shir narrated to me: Sulaymn ibn arb narrated to us: ammd ibn Zayd narrated to us: Ayyb said: 
                . 
I have a neighbour, and he is a man of virtue, but if he were to testify for me about two dates, I would not consider his testimony acceptable.

Al-fidh said in the biography of Al-Abbs ibn Mabb, known as Ibn Shnah: Maslamah ibn Qsim said:
       .
He is weak in adth; his narrations should not be written. He was a friend of mine. [Lisn al-Mzn (3/244)]

Source:
